chief_complaint: A 70-year-old Chinese male presented with hematemesis and melena.
history: The patient was admitted to the department of gastroenterology with complaints of hematemesis and melena. He had a history of arterial embolization and presented with a rare, delayed complication of coil migration. There is no consensus on the treatment of gastrointestinal bleeding due to coil migration.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings are not explicitly stated in the case report.
labs: ECG, bloods, imaging, and other tests are not explicitly stated in the case report.
correct_diagnosis: Gastrointestinal hemorrhage due to a fistula between a hepatic aneurysm and a duodenal bulb ulcer, precipitated by spring coil migration.
differentials: Gastrointestinal hemorrhage due to a fistula between a hepatic aneurysm and a duodenal bulb ulcer, precipitated by spring coil migration.
Duodenal ulcer bleeding due to other causes
Gastrointestinal hemorrhage due to a hepatic aneurysm rupture
Gastrointestinal hemorrhage due to a duodenal bulb ulcer
